signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD/CHF at 0.7156 — NZD leads G10 performance (+1.509%) while CHF lags (-0.651%), creating a 2.160% spread. The divergence is fundamentally anchored by the RBNZ 2.5% (HIKING) vs SNB 0.0% (ON HOLD) stance, yielding a +250bp carry advantage for NZD. RBNZ 2.5%.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.83 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, but price is now testing levels above the 160.00 threshold where BoJ verbal warnings previously intensified. Options market is pricing heavy protection against a reversal with a -1.28 25d RR put skew and an elevated VRP of +3.6% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.5%). BoJ 1.0%.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Positioning + CESI + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4166 — COT LF net short -92,861 (20.6% long) aligns with a negative CESI (-8), signaling persistent CAD data disappointment. Retail sentiment is 83% short, diverging from the institutional short-CAD/long-USD bias. This retail fade at extreme levels increases the risk of a squeeze higher if the June jobs report confirms the expected slowdown. Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% provides a +150bp carry tailwind for the pair.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Vol Premium + G10 Outperformance
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3413 — GBP is the second strongest G10 currency (+0.663%) supported by a positive CESI (+22). Options market shows extreme hedging demand with a +5.5% VRP (IV 11.0% vs HV30 5.5%), the highest in the G10 space. While carry is neutral (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), the real rate differential (+0.78% GBP vs +1.47% USD) suggests USD remains structurally more restrictive, capping GBP upside despite the data beats.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Vol Complacency + Gold Correlation
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6949 — The pair shows unusual vol complacency with a negative VRP of -3.8% (IV 3.1% vs HV30 6.8%). AUD/USD remains highly correlated to Gold (corr=+0.886, z=+1.02σ), making the pair vulnerable to the current Gold retreat toward $4,100. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry cushion, but thin options supply suggests the market is underpricing potential downside volatility.
